## What's fixed

A bug in the 0.2.1 strike-map sweep prevented the dynamic blocklist from ever firing. This release restores the intended behaviour.

### The bug

`ExpireCandidatesLocked` captured `now = NowNs()` at the top, then called `RecordStrikeLocked` per expired candidate; that recorder used its own — slightly later — `NowNs()` call to stamp `first_strike_ns`. The strike-map sweep at the bottom of the same function then compared the older outer `now` against the newer `first_strike_ns` with `uint64_t` arithmetic, which underflowed to ~`UINT64_MAX`, always satisfied the staleness check, and erased the strike entry that had been recorded microseconds earlier.

Visible effect: `drop_relearn_unconfirmed` kept advancing, but the per-source strike count in `r->strikes[ip_h].count` never accumulated past 1 — so the 2-strike / 60-s policy (and the 5-strike / 1-h, and the 10-strike / 24-h) could never trigger. Forged-handshake sources struck the relay forever without escalation.

### The fix

Refresh `sweep_now = NowNs()` after the strike-recording loop and use it for both the inversion guard and the staleness check. The `sweep_now > first_strike_ns` guard is belt-and-suspenders against any future caller that might invert the ordering again.

### Verification

Deterministic 2-burst reproducer, single source, 35-s gap between forges:

| build | strikes | blocklist | verdict |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| `edfa8bb` (pre-cfecbcd, pre-bug) | 2 | fired @+31s | ✓ |
| `0.2.1` (cfecbcd merged, bug active) | 2 | did NOT fire | ✗ |
| `0.2.2` (this fix) | 2 | fired @+31s | ✓ |

The reproducer + bug analysis are in PR #20.

### Upgrading

Drop-in. No config or roster changes. Existing `0.2.1` deployments should `apt install --reinstall hyper-derp` (or apt upgrade once a tagged release is published) and restart the service. Operators who previously saw `drop_relearn_unconfirmed` advancing without corresponding `wg-relay blocklist …` warn lines were hitting this bug; post-upgrade those should appear when forged handshakes meet the 2/60-s threshold.

### No other behaviour changes

`0.2.2` is the strike-sweep fix and nothing else. The 0.2.1 wg-relay hardening features (shape filter, MAC1 verification, automatic roaming, blocklist) are unchanged in scope and design.
